mission.title: Install and Configure Argo Rollouts (Argo) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Argo Rollouts is a Kubernetes controller that provides advanced deployment capabilities such as blue-green and canary deployments. Installing Argo Rollouts allows you to implement progressive delivery strategies, enhancing the reliability and control of your application updates.

steps.title: Create the Argo Rollouts namespace
steps.description:
Create a dedicated namespace for Argo Rollouts to operate in. This helps in organizing resources and managing permissions effectively.
```bash
kubectl create namespace argo-rollouts
```

steps.title: Install Argo Rollouts using Helm
steps.description:
Add the Argo Rollouts Helm repository and install the chart with a specific version.
```bash
helm repo add argo https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm
helm repo update
helm install argo-rollouts argo/argo-rollouts --version 1.8.4 --namespace argo-rollouts --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Argo Rollouts controller pod is running in the namespace you created.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-rollouts
```

steps.title: Configure RBAC for Argo Rollouts
steps.description:
Apply the necessary Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) settings to ensure that the Argo Rollouts can manage resources effectively.
```bash
kubectl apply -n argo-rollouts -f https://github.com/argoproj/argo-rollouts/releases/latest/download/namespace-install.yaml
```

steps.title: Access the Argo Rollouts dashboard
steps.description:
Port-forward the Argo Rollouts dashboard service to access it locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/argo-rollouts-dashboard -n argo-rollouts 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Argo Rollouts controller running in the 'argo-rollouts' namespace, and you will be able to access the dashboard at http://localhost:8080. You can also verify that the necessary RBAC permissions are applied.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add argo https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm
helm repo update
helm install argo-rollouts argo/argo-rollouts --version 1.8.4 --namespace argo-rollouts --create-namespace

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description: Uninstall the Argo Rollouts Helm release to remove the deployment from your cluster.

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description: Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent resources associated with Argo Rollouts.

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description: Delete the namespace used for Argo Rollouts and verify that all resources have been cleaned up.

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description: Before upgrading, back up the current state of your Argo Rollouts configuration.

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description: Update the Helm repository and execute the upgrade command to install the latest version of Argo Rollouts.

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description: Check that the Argo Rollouts controller pod is running the new version in the specified namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This issue occurs when the Argo Rollouts controller pod fails to start repeatedly. Check the logs for errors using the command: 
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n argo-rollouts
```
Fix any configuration issues or resource limitations identified in the logs.

troubleshooting.title: Argo Rollouts dashboard not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Argo Rollouts dashboard, ensure that the port-forward command is running. Verify the service is up with: 
```bash
kubectl get svc -n argo-rollouts
```
Restart the port-forward command if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: RBAC permissions issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter permission errors, check the RBAC configuration. Review the applied roles and bindings with: 
```bash
kubectl get roles,rolebindings -n argo-rollouts
```
Adjust the RBAC settings as needed.

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade command fails, check the output for specific error messages. You can roll back to the previous version with: 
```bash
helm rollback argo-rollouts <previous-version> --namespace argo-rollouts
```
Investigate the error messages for further troubleshooting.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
